title: Weather Today [06 April, 2026]: Check IMD Rain Predictions, Snow Alert, Thunderstorms & Heat Across Delhi-NCR, Mumbai, Chennai, Karnataka, Rajasthan, Jammu, Ahmedabad, Punjab, Hyderabad, Uttar Pradesh - The Sunday Guardian
full_text:
According to the India Meteorological Department (IMD), several parts of India may experience rain, thunderstorms, lightning, and gusty winds on April 6, 2026, especially across Delhi-NCR, Uttar Pradesh, Rajasthan, and Punjab. Meanwhile, Mumbai, Chennai, and Ahmedabad are likely to see warm and humid conditions, while higher Himalayan regions of Jammu & Kashmir could receive light snowfall due to a western disturbance.
India is witnessing a mix of rain, thunderstorms, gusty winds, and rising summer heat across several regions today, according to the latest updates from the India Meteorological Department (IMD). Weather conditions on April 6, 2026 are being influenced by a western disturbance in North India and moisture-laden winds in central and southern regions, which could trigger scattered rainfall and storm activity in multiple states.
Several major cities including Delhi-NCR, Mumbai, Chennai, Hyderabad, Ahmedabad, and parts of Uttar Pradesh and Rajasthan may experience changing weather conditions throughout the day. Thunderstorms accompanied by lightning and gusty winds are also possible in some areas.
Weather systems affecting northern India may bring rain and even snowfall in higher Himalayan regions, particularly in parts of Jammu & Kashmir and nearby mountainous areas. Western disturbances moving across the region are expected to trigger cloud cover, rainfall, and possible snow at higher altitudes, while lower valleys may receive light rain.
Such conditions could temporarily reduce temperatures and bring relief from early summer heat in northern plains.
North India may see rapidly changing weather today, with cloudy skies, thunderstorms, and occasional showers forecast across Delhi, Uttar Pradesh, Punjab, and Rajasthan. Authorities have also warned about lightning and strong winds during storm activity. These weather systems may lower daytime temperatures slightly in several northern cities.
Residents of Delhi-NCR can expect partly cloudy skies with chances of thunderstorms and rain during the day. Strong winds and lightning are also possible as an active western disturbance moves across the region. Temperatures may remain slightly below seasonal averages due to cloud cover and intermittent showers.
Chennai continues to experience hot and humid conditions, with daytime temperatures hovering around the mid-30s Celsius. Meteorologists warn that heatwave-like conditions may develop during April, although occasional rainfall could provide temporary relief. Residents are advised to stay hydrated and avoid prolonged exposure to the afternoon sun.
In Mumbai, weather conditions are expected to remain warm and humid, with partly cloudy skies. While widespread rain is unlikely, isolated showers cannot be ruled out due to changing wind patterns along the western coast. Temperatures are likely to remain in the low to mid-30s°C range, typical for early summer.
Hyderabad may experience partly cloudy skies with chances of thunderstorms later in the day. Weather systems affecting southern India could trigger brief rain spells accompanied by lightning and gusty winds in some parts of Telangana.
Several areas in Karnataka, particularly interior districts, may see scattered rain and thunderstorm activity due to atmospheric instability and moisture inflow. Cloudy skies may also help moderate daytime temperatures.
Parts of Uttar Pradesh are expected to witness light rain, thunderstorms, and gusty winds today. The IMD has advised residents to remain cautious during lightning activity and sudden storms. These conditions may temporarily reduce heat levels in some districts.
Weather in Rajasthan could remain partly cloudy with isolated thunderstorms, especially in eastern and northern districts. Gusty winds and lightning may accompany rain activity in some locations.
Higher regions of Jammu and Kashmir may experience rainfall and snowfall in mountainous areas, while lower regions could see cloudy skies and cooler temperatures due to the ongoing western disturbance.
Ahmedabad is likely to remain hot and dry during the day, with temperatures gradually rising as summer strengthens in western India. However, localised clouds or brief showers cannot be ruled out in nearby regions.
In Punjab, weather conditions may include cloud cover, thunderstorms, and occasional rainfall, similar to neighboring northern states. Strong winds and lightning may occur during storm activity.
Despite scattered rainfall, summer heat continues to intensify in several parts of India, particularly in western and southern regions.
The IMD’s seasonal outlook suggests that April could witness above-normal rainfall nationwide, which may delay the onset of extreme heat in some regions, even as certain coastal and central areas remain warm.
For the coming week, Delhi-NCR may experience alternating periods of sunshine, cloud cover, and thunderstorms. Rain activity may increase around April 7–8, accompanied by gusty winds.
Temperatures are expected to remain slightly below normal due to recurring weather disturbances.
Delhi’s air quality today is expected to remain in the moderate category, with improving conditions due to strong winds and possible rainfall that may disperse pollutants.
